title: High DPI Support
author: donebydavid
body_snippet:
Apparently #1107 was merged to fix issues with High DPI support as mentioned in ticket #321.  On v1.77.1.27654 this issue is still occurring where by the mRemote interface scales fine, but the remote connection does not.

## Expected Behavior
Remote session to scale correctly

## Current Behaviour
Remote session does not scale

iterations.description: RDP sessions don't inherit client DPI scaling factor — the DesktopScaleFactor property exists but is not auto-detected from the current monitor's DPI

comments.author: wilber82
comments.snippet: Same issue. My laptop is 4K screen. Changed to high resolution and scaled 125%. mRemoteNG interface is fine scaled. But Windows remote desktop inside mRemoteNG is not scaled properly.

comments.author: znbrain
comments.snippet: Same issue. When I use mstsc.exe the remote desktop ist scaled to 125% (like the Host) too. In mRemoteNG the Scalingfactor ist always set to 100%.

comments.author: changbowen
comments.snippet: Would be nice if mRemoteNG can use the same scaling factor as the client environment.

comments.author: changbowen
comments.snippet:
Based on the modifications by @tecxx I adapted the changes to the current stable version 1.76 because 1.77 didn't like my KeePass and auto type just won't work.
You can find a portable version release [here](https://github.com/changbowen/mRemoteNG/releases/tag/v1.76.21).

notes:
AI triage (claude): RDP sessions don't inherit client DPI scaling factor — the DesktopScaleFactor property exists but is not auto-detected from the current monitor's DPI
Approach: Auto-detect current monitor DPI via GetDpiForMonitor and pass matching DesktopScaleFactor to RDP ActiveX control at connect time; add an 'Auto' option to RDPDesktopScaleFactor enum that reads the client DPI dynamically
